commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes heartbeat active-hours seeking to use a one-million-iteration cap and adds sub-minute phase-alignment regression tests.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +68. Total +70 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blocker: source inspection shows accepted millisecond heartbeat intervals flow into runner scheduling, and a read-only timing probe reproduced the expensive production predicate shape. The contributor also provided terminal proof for the narrow-window correctness case.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a concrete narrow blocker: preserve sub-minute phase correctness while bounding production active-hours predicate cost before merge.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): remove static iteration cap in seekNextActivePhaseDueMs
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agents configured with sub-minute heartbeat intervals
(\`every: 30s\`, \`every: 10s\`, etc.) and active hours windows would
permanently stop running heartbeats when the current time fell outside the
active window.

The root cause: \`seekNextActivePhaseDueMs\` had a static iteration cap
of 10,080 (calibrated for 1-minute intervals). When \`intervalMs < 60_000\`,
the cap was reached before the 7-day time horizon, so the seek returned
\`startMs\` — a time outside active hours. The heartbeat then busy-looped
without ever reaching the next active window.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix replaces the static 10,080 cap with a 1M-iteration safety cap while
keeping per-candidate checking (step = \`intervalMs\`, no batching or
skipping).

The 1M cap:
- Prevents event-loop stalls from sub-ms intervals (~2 ms runtime in V8)
- Covers the full 7-day horizon for any \`intervalMs >= 605ms\`
- Preserves phase alignment: every candidate is \`startMs + N * intervalMs\`

Earlier iterations of this fix tried batched stepping but were rejected because
any multiplier > 1 can skip phase slots inside narrow active windows (e.g.,
59s interval / 1min window).

## User Impact

Users with sub-minute heartbeat intervals and active hours can now correctly
seek into the next active window regardless of how far away it is.
No impact on users with minute-or-larger intervals (multiplier = 1).

## Evidence

**Unit tests (17/17 pass):** 14 existing + 3 new
- 30s interval / 09:00–17:00 active: seeks to 09:00 next day, phase-aligned
- 59s interval / 1min narrow window (09:00–09:01): finds 09:00:43 (977 steps, integer)
- 1ms interval / never-active: bounded at 1M iterations, under 50ms

**Real behavior proof (\`node --import tsx\` importing actual module):**

\`\`\`
=== seekNextActivePhaseDueMs v4 (per-candidate, 1M-iter cap) ===

1. 30s interval, 09:00-17:00, start 2026-01-01 17:00:00 UTC
   Result: 2026-01-02 09:00:00 UTC (in active: true)

2. 59s interval, narrow 09:00-09:01 window, start 2026-01-01 17:00:00 UTC
   Result: 2026-01-02 09:00:43 UTC (in active: true)
   Steps: 977 (integer: true)

3. 7s interval, always-active, start 2026-01-01 12:00:00 UTC
   Result: 2026-01-01 12:00:00 UTC (= startMs: true)

4. 1s interval, never-active, start 2026-01-01 12:00:00 UTC
   Result: 2026-01-01 12:00:00 UTC (= startMs: true)
   Elapsed: 12.2ms

5. computeNextHeartbeatPhaseDueMs (30s), now 2026-06-25 10:00:00 UTC
   Next due: 2026-06-25 10:00:30 UTC

6. Full-path: 30s heartbeat, 20:00 -> seek into 09-17 active
   Phase: 15000ms, Next raw due: 2026-06-25 20:00:15 UTC
   Seek result: 2026-06-26 09:00:15 UTC (in active: true)

=== All 6 scenarios passed ===
\`\`\`

**CI:** checks-node-compact-small-whole-* (10 shards): 9 passed, 1 flaky
(unrelated \`test/wildcards\` test, confirmed intermittent). All
heartbeat-schedule tests pass across all shards.
target.author: wendy-chsy

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
